name: Yazid ibn al-Mughaffal
title: Hero of Ju'fi
shortDescription: A Shia and brave companion of Amir al-Mu'minin (AS) who joined Imam Hussain (AS) in Karbala and was valiantly martyred.
fullStory:
Yazid ibn al-Mughaffal al-Ju'fi was a loyal, brave companion, and poet of Amir al-Mu'minin Ali (AS) who had shown great heroism in the battles of Jamal and Siffin.

When Imam Hussain (AS) reached Karbala, Yazid ibn al-Mughaffal also made his way to the Imam's camp. Being a pious man and a formidable swordsman, he entered the battlefield on Ashura with the Imam's permission.

He recited an epic poem on the battlefield, introducing himself: 'I am Yazid ibn al-Mughaffal, I attack you with a sharp sword and defend Hussain, that pure-hearted man.'

Yazid struck the Kufan ranks with unparalleled skill and, after a long battle and killing a number of enemies, finally fell to the ground with a body full of wounds and attained the great honor of martyrdom.
quoteArabic: أَنا يزِيدُ بْنُ مُغَفَّلٍ... أَعْلُو بِسَيْفِي هامَةَ المُجَهَّلِ...
quote: (In his war poetry:) I am Yazid ibn al-Mughaffal... I strike the heads of the ignorant with my sword and defend the pure-hearted Hussain.
